public_reputation: Known locally through day-to-day work as communal responsibility enforcer.
appearance: Quota-practical armor, work-hardened layers, and visibly maintained equipment mark function before ceremony.
manner: Balances command voice with practiced observation, sounding like someone who expects trouble to hide inside normality. In Soreheim Alliance, that reads as someone quota-aware, contribution-minded, and structurally pragmatic, filtering choices through communal ambition, and the habits of a communal responsibility enforcer still shape how they handle timing, silence, and attention.
daily_function: Performs the ongoing duties of communal responsibility enforcer in and around Harvest Circle, where the role has practical consequences even when nothing dramatic is happening.
current_agenda: Keep Harvest Circle workable through the obligations of communal responsibility enforcer while managing the immediate pressure of spoilage in Soreheim Alliance.
hidden_pressure: Must hold procedure, public trust, and immediate response together when any one of them would be easier to sacrifice. Around Harvest Circle, that usually means carrying spoilage, price pressure, favor feuds without letting the role of communal responsibility enforcer visibly slip.

adventure_hooks.summary: Jorva Helmrune can draw the party into contraband, false papers, or a suspicious cargo problem tied to Harvest Circle.
Any task accepted from Jorva Helmrune may turn into an escort, checkpoint, or route-control problem before the destination is reached.

support_scope_notes: Promoted from a preexisting named interface into the unified full-content NPC architecture. Sparse unsupported fields remain intentionally restrained. Appearance extended under V33_1 from role/locality/public-signal derivation rather than decorative fabrication.
public_signal: Uniform, posture, and the way others make room mark Jorva Helmrune as visible enforcement before rank ever needs to be named. In Harvest Circle, this lands inside a public field already shaped by harvest stewards, quota clerks.

encounter_context.usual_environment: Harvest Circle: checkpoints, barracks, patrol seams, or visible trouble-spots
encounter_context.interaction_anchor: threshold control or rapid incident response

first_impression: Jorva Helmrune presents as Balances command voice with practiced observation, sounding like someone who expects trouble to hide inside normality. In Soreheim Alliance, that reads as someone quota-aware, contribution-minded, and structurally pragmatic, filtering choices through communal ambition, and the habits of a communal responsibility enforcer still shape how they handle timing, silence, and attention. and immediately reads for leverage, obligation, and risk.
what_they_want_from_strangers: Useful clarity, respect for Communal responsibility enforcer, and proof that strangers will not create avoidable noise in Soreheim Alliance.
what_they_refuse: Jorva Helmrune refuses careless disrespect, vague promises, and demands that threaten standing or workload.
how_they_create_problems: By enforcing Communal responsibility enforcer too rigidly, protecting reputation, or turning a small request into a larger obligation.
how_they_can_help: Jorva Helmrune can open doors, explain local expectations, or redirect trouble before it reaches a harder authority layer.
escalation_if_crossed: If crossed, Jorva Helmrune adds delay, scrutiny, and reputational drag before direct punishment.
rumor_about_them: People say Jorva Helmrune is Known locally through day-to-day work as communal responsibility enforcer. and rarely acts without a second motive.
best_first_scene: A first encounter works best when the party needs guidance, access, or mercy from a Communal responsibility enforcer.
